steam doesn’t require steam hardware to play games on it, so valve doesn’t sell massive amounts of hardware, and doesn’t get discounts from production companies on that hardware.
for people playing mostly older games in steam its still the cheapest handheld that has an oled screen. old games run perfectly so its not needed to spend more for a newer device.

my plan is to step away from the whole pc handheld craze after getting the new xbox ally x20. got a 2tb limited edition sd oled (transluscent design) for $500 off market years ago . now i can have my secondary more heavy hitter pc handheld in oled as well. currently rocking a 2024 ally x for those more intensive games so it would be an (albeit expensive) upgrade regardless. 2 limited editions sounds like a nice series finale with this hobby.

yeah makes me sick. i praised valve for the price of the steam deck at launch. now i can't even recommend it to anyone and i don't love the thought of being priced out of buying a replacement if my current one ever dies. i am fully committed to allowing the world to price me out of everything. phones, streaming services, concerts, collectibles, video games , you name it. the price of living a "normal" life is going to become only for the truly wealthy and i'm already making moves in my life to cut these things off so that when we do legitimately get priced out of all of it, i won't feel the difference because i've already made my clean break away. pray for me and i pray for all those who struggle financially during these trying times.
